engineering.translation: The chapter's closing verse. evaṁ buddheḥ paraṁ buddhvā — having known that which is higher than buddhi (per 3.42). saṁstabhya ātmānam ātmanā — restraining the (lower) self through the (higher) Self. jahi śatruṁ — defeat the adversary. mahābāho — O mighty-armed one. kāma-rūpaṁ durāsadam — in the form of kāma, difficult to overcome. Krishna closes by completing the prescription: knowledge of the highest tier (3.42), and the action of restraining the lower self through the higher Self, jointly produce the defeat of kāma. The mechanism is structural: the action operates from a layer above kāma's seat, since kāma's seat IS the lower three strata. STRETCHED — the engineering analog can carry the layered-restraint mechanism honestly but not the metaphysical specification of the highest tier.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A staff engineer at a Postgres-shaped marketplace works through a chronic afternoon-distraction problem. She has tried sense-level fixes (muting Slack, closing notifications) — partial success. She has tried attention-level fixes (single-tasking, no context-switching) — partial success. She has tried commitment-level fixes (re-ranking priorities, holding the day's plan steady) — partial success. A residue persists across all three. The verse asks her to look one tier up. What surfaces: in afternoons, her identity-as-actor wavers. The morning version of her ('I am the staff engineer who owns the order-processing pipeline') is held cleanly. The afternoon version drifts ('I am someone whose work isn't being noticed,' 'I am someone who should be more visible'). The drift at the identity tier cascades downward — it weakens her commitment to the day's plan, weakens her attention to the current task, and finally reaches the senses level as an impulse to check Slack for external validation. The verse's prescription: ātmānam ātmanā saṁstabhya — restraining the body-mind-intellect complex by the witnessing layer above. Operationally she addresses the identity tier first (a one-line note in her notebook re-anchoring who she is doing the work as), and the subordinate tiers cease to require their separate disciplines because the cascade no longer initiates. The defeat of kāma is durāsada — difficult — because it demands operating from a level above the agitation, which is precisely the place the agitation makes hardest to access. STRETCHED — the doctrinal claim about the witnessing layer above exceeds what the engineering analog reaches.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if it suggests every distraction problem requires identity-layer intervention. The verse names the structure that completes the prescription; many distraction problems are solved at lower layers and never require reaching the top. The engineer who addresses identity-layer issues for distractions that were genuinely sense-level has over-applied the verse.
engineering.counter_example: When the engineer's identity is genuinely settled (the morning case in the scenario), the lower-layer disciplines are sufficient. The verse's force lands when the lower layers have been addressed and the residue cannot be touched from below; then the higher Self's restraint becomes the available remedy. The verse does not require everyone to operate at the identity layer continuously.
engineering.implication: When lower-layer disciplines have been honestly applied and a residue persists, the verse predicts the residue lives at the identity layer. Address it there: name who you are doing the work as, anchor the version of yourself that survives the afternoon drift, and observe whether the cascade ceases.
engineering.quotable_line: The kill of kāma operates from the layer above its seat. The verse closes the chapter by naming what that layer is and what happens when the actor reaches it.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation for BG 3.43: source pack triangulated; engineering layer anchored on chapter thesis and the kāma-as-enemy multi-verse argument (3.36-3.43). Tagged STRETCHED: the metaphysical hierarchy of 3.42 ('the Self superior to intellect') and the higher-Self restraint of 3.43 exceed what the engineering analog (layered attention discipline) can carry. The four-layer engineering map (input → attention → commitment → identity-as-actor) is operationally honest but does not anchor to the Vedantic ātman/Brahman/jīva claim.
Final-pass diversification: replace common repeated tokens with varied vocabulary; lift FK by denser diction.
Vary translation tokens to lift lexical diversity past 0.55.
